justifications.completeness: Extraction omits all annotated persons and vehicles and therefore fails to capture required objects.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides some descriptive scene context about buildings but lacks nearly all semantic attributes for persons and vehicles such as actions, motion status, and openings.
justifications.spatial_relations: Mentions left and right placement of buildings but misses spatial relations for pedestrians and vehicles and relations to the road.
justifications.overall: Overall the extraction gives a moderately detailed scene description of static structures but completely misses object-level detections and semantics, yielding very low utility for driving perception.

justifications.completeness: Detected only two vehicles and no pedestrians, missing most annotated objects (2 of 7), so completeness is low.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides useful vehicle attributes (type, motion_status, trunk/doors, spatial relations) for the reported objects but omits pedestrian semantics and many vehicles.
justifications.spatial_relations: Offers plausible spatial relations for the reported vehicles (left side, in the distance) but is somewhat vague and omits relations for most ground-truth objects.
justifications.overall: Captures a small subset of scene elements with reasonable detail for those detected but misses pedestrians and multiple vehicles, resulting in a low overall score.

justifications.completeness: The extraction captures one vehicle and a basic scene description but entirely omits all four pedestrians and the majority of vehicles from the ground truth, so coverage is very low.
justifications.semantic_richness: Some object-level attributes are provided (vehicle type, motion_status, trunk/doors), but many entities and contextual/person action details from the ground truth are missing, limiting richness.
justifications.spatial_relations: A single spatial relation for the truck is given, but spatial relations for other vehicles and any relations involving pedestrians are absent, yielding poor spatial characterization.
justifications.overall: Overall the extraction contains a few correct facts but misses most entities and relations from the ground truth, resulting in low practical utility.

justifications.completeness: The extraction reports only one vehicle and no pedestrians, missing four people and four of five annotated vehicles.
justifications.semantic_richness: It captures basic vehicle attributes (type, motion status, trunk and doors) and one spatial relation but omits pedestrian actions, counts, identities, and most vehicle instances and motions.
justifications.spatial_relations: It correctly states a truck is parked on the left but provides no spatial relations for the pedestrians or the other vehicles, so coverage is limited.
justifications.overall: The extraction identifies a single vehicle with some attributes but fails to detect pedestrians and most vehicles, resulting in low overall usefulness.

justifications.completeness: Extraction misses all three pedestrians and several vehicles present in the ground truth, reporting only two vehicles.
justifications.semantic_richness: Extraction provides some vehicle attributes but includes incorrect open-door/trunk states and omits pedestrian actions and ages, so semantic detail is largely incomplete or wrong.
justifications.spatial_relations: Only a single vague relation for the truck is given while spatial information for pedestrians and other vehicles is missing, reducing spatial coverage and utility.
justifications.overall: Overall the extraction is poor: it omits pedestrians, misses most vehicles, and contains incorrect attribute values, yielding low reliability for driving perception tasks.

justifications.completeness: Detected only two vehicles and no pedestrians while ground truth contains four persons and six vehicles, so coverage is very low.
justifications.semantic_richness: Extracted a few attributes (motion status, doors) but missed rich person attributes (actions, age, carrying) and many vehicle instances, giving low semantic detail.
justifications.spatial_relations: Provided simple spatial cues for two objects but omitted relations for multiple people and vehicles and used vague terms like "in the distance", so spatial information is sparse and partially unhelpful.
justifications.overall: The extraction is largely incomplete and lacks semantic depth and comprehensive spatial coverage, making it unreliable for autonomous driving perception tasks.

justifications.completeness: The model detected no pedestrians or vehicles despite the ground truth listing six persons and seven vehicles, so completeness is essentially zero.
justifications.semantic_richness: A single high-level scene description was provided but no object-level attributes, actions, or transport states from the ground truth were captured, giving very low richness.
justifications.spatial_relations: No spatial relations, placements, or interactions between agents and vehicles were reported, so spatial relation information is absent.
justifications.overall: The extraction misses all annotated agents and vehicles and lacks attributes or spatial relations, resulting in a very low overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Detected only one pedestrian and no vehicles, missing six annotated persons and all vehicles, so coverage is very low.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides a few scene details but omits many attributes present in the ground truth such as multiple actors, transporting and communicative cues, so richness is limited.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes generic relations for the single detected pedestrian which may be plausible but fails to capture spatial relations for other actors and the specific illegal crossing context, so only partial credit is warranted.
justifications.overall: Extraction captures one plausible person and a descriptive scene element but fails on multiple actors, vehicle detection, and several key attributes, resulting in poor overall performance.

justifications.completeness: Detects only one pedestrian and one vehicle while ground truth contains seven pedestrians and four vehicles, so it misses the majority of objects and attributes.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides some descriptive details like clothing and building that add richness, but it omits many per-agent annotations from the ground truth and mislabels contextual elements such as pedestrian crossing and vehicle motion.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes two spatial cues for the single detected person but these are unverified, and it fails to provide spatial or motion relations for the numerous other pedestrians and vehicles present in the ground truth.
justifications.overall: Extraction offers a few useful descriptive items but is largely incomplete and contains incorrect or missing labels for motion and context, resulting in low overall fidelity to the ground truth.

justifications.completeness: Detected 1 of 5 pedestrians and no vehicles, missing multiple people and all vehicle annotations.
justifications.semantic_richness: For the single detected pedestrian the model provided several semantic attributes correctly (action, age, simple context), but it failed to capture the other persons and some transport/communicative details in the ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: The extraction includes a spatial relation for the detected person but it conflicts with its simple_context wording and omits spatial relations for the remaining pedestrians and vehicles.
justifications.overall: Overall the extraction captures one correct pedestrian with reasonable labels but misses most entities and many spatial and semantic details from the ground truth.

justifications.completeness: Detected only one of seven pedestrians and one of three vehicles, so most objects are missing.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides multiple attribute types (actions, context, communicative state, vehicle status) but misses many per-object labels and some attributes appear incorrect.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes spatial tags for the reported pedestrian and vehicle which align with some context, but spatial relations are absent for most objects and position accuracy is uncertain.
justifications.overall: Low object recall and incomplete attribute coverage produce an overall poor extraction despite a few correct attribute predictions.

justifications.completeness: Detected none of the seven pedestrians and four vehicles present in the ground truth.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides a correct high-level scene description but contains no semantic attributes for objects such as actions, ages, or vehicle states.
justifications.spatial_relations: Offers general scene layout like buildings on both sides but no spatial relations between road users or relative positions of pedestrians and vehicles.
justifications.overall: Overall the extraction is largely incomplete and lacks object-level semantics and spatial relations, reflected by a very low aggregate score.

justifications.completeness: The extraction reports only one pedestrian and one vehicle while the ground truth contains five persons and five vehicles, so coverage is very low.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction includes multiple attribute fields (action, context, communicative, transporting, age) showing richness, but it omits most entities and contains at least one incorrect transporting label, reducing reliability.
justifications.spatial_relations: Provided spatial relations are specific for the reported objects and the van matches the scene description, but relations are missing for most objects and the pedestrian side placement may be incorrect.
justifications.overall: The output gives a somewhat detailed description for a single pedestrian and one vehicle but is incomplete across entities and contains errors, limiting its overall usefulness.

justifications.completeness: Detected scene context and one vehicle but missed all seven pedestrians and most vehicles in the ground truth.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provided a few attributes for a single vehicle (motion, trunk, doors) but omitted pedestrian actions, ages, and most vehicle states.
justifications.spatial_relations: Included one left-side parking relation but failed to localize or relate multiple vehicles and all pedestrians in the scene.
justifications.overall: Captures a basic scene gist and one vehicle but largely omits pedestrians and many correct vehicle labels and relations, yielding poor overall coverage.

justifications.completeness: Detected one pedestrian and no vehicles while ground truth has four pedestrians and four vehicles, so most objects are missing.
justifications.semantic_richness: Captures multiple attributes (action, context, age, spatial relation) for the detected person but misses other actors and contains contradictory context, limiting richness.
justifications.spatial_relations: Provides a spatial relation ('in the middle of the road') but contradicts its own simple_context and fails to annotate the other jaywalking pedestrians, so spatial coverage and consistency are poor.
justifications.overall: Extraction is sparse and partially inconsistent, detecting one pedestrian with mixed attributes and omitting all vehicles and most persons, yielding low overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Only two of four pedestrians were detected and no vehicles were reported, so a majority of annotated objects are missing.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction includes several attribute fields but misses key labels like jaywalking, mislabels age for one person, and omits vehicle attributes entirely.
justifications.spatial_relations: Some spatial relations are provided for the two detected pedestrians but they are incomplete or imprecise relative to ground truth and absent for all vehicles.
justifications.overall: Due to multiple missed objects, an age misclassification, and missing vehicle information the overall extraction quality is low.

justifications.completeness: Misses all annotated persons and vehicles, only provides a generic scene context.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides descriptive context about the street and signage but contains no object-level attributes, actions, or counts present in the ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: No spatial relations or placements of objects are reported despite multiple annotated pedestrians and vehicles.
justifications.overall: Captures high-level appearance but fails to detect or describe any annotated people or vehicles, making it largely insufficient for driving perception tasks.

justifications.completeness: Detected one of four pedestrians and no vehicles, missing three people and two vehicles.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides several attributes for the detected pedestrian (action, context, age, spatial relation) but omits other pedestrians and vehicle attributes, limiting overall richness.
justifications.spatial_relations: Gives a spatial relation for the detected pedestrian that aligns with being at the road side but introduces an unverified left-side detail and omits spatial info for other agents.
justifications.overall: Extraction captures one accurate pedestrian instance with reasonable attribute detail but misses most actors and all vehicles, yielding low overall performance.

justifications.completeness: Detected only one of six people and missed all vehicles, so coverage is very low.
justifications.semantic_richness: Extracted a useful set of attributes for the detected person (action, context, age, spatial relation) but failed to annotate multiple actors and vehicles.
justifications.spatial_relations: Provides a specific relation for the single detected pedestrian but fails to capture the on-road versus sidewalk placements of other pedestrians and any vehicle relations.
justifications.overall: Good attribute schema for a single actor but poor recall of actors and missing vehicles result in low overall utility.

justifications.completeness: Misses all pedestrians and only lists three malformed vehicle entries while ground truth has five vehicles and two persons.
justifications.semantic_richness: Includes vehicle attributes and spatial relations but many attributes are misspelled or malformed and it omits person attributes and object IDs.
justifications.spatial_relations: Provides one valid left-side relation but other spatial entries are invalid frame references and lacks relations for most vehicles and pedestrians.
justifications.overall: Extraction is low-quality: it misses pedestrians, has malformed vehicle records, and only minimal valid spatial info.

justifications.completeness: Detected only one of two pedestrians and only two of four vehicles, omitting several object instances and some attributes.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides basic action and status labels but misses important context such as jaywalking, the bicyclist/sitting annotation, object track ids, and several vehicle instances and properties.
justifications.spatial_relations: Only a single vague pedestrian relation is given while spatial relations for other actors are absent and the pedestrian crossing context is misrepresented.
justifications.overall: Captures a few low level labels correctly but misses multiple entities and key contextual semantics, yielding poor alignment with the ground truth.

justifications.completeness: Misses both pedestrians from the ground truth and detects only one vehicle out of three, so coverage of objects is very low.
justifications.semantic_richness: Reports basic vehicle attributes for a single object but omits person actions, contexts, and most vehicle instances and attributes, yielding low semantic detail.
justifications.spatial_relations: Provides a single, likely incorrect relation ('inside the vehicle') and fails to situate multiple objects relative to the scene or ego vehicle.
justifications.overall: Overall the extraction is sparse and partially incorrect, missing key actors and many vehicles while providing unreliable spatial information.

justifications.completeness: Extraction failed to report any of the annotated persons or vehicles from the ground truth.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides some scene-level description of the street and buildings but omits all person actions, ages, and vehicle states present in the ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: No spatial relationships or positional information between objects are provided.
justifications.overall: Overall performance is poor because the extraction only offers limited scene description while missing all annotated dynamic agents and their attributes or spatial context.

justifications.completeness: The extraction reports only one pedestrian and one vehicle while the ground truth contains two people and multiple vehicles, so it misses the majority of objects.
justifications.semantic_richness: Some attributes are present (atomic action, age, motion status) and the walking adult is correct, but the model omits the cyclist, misrepresents vehicle properties (trunk/doors on a bicycle), and ignores several vehicles, reducing overall semantic detail and correctness.
justifications.spatial_relations: The extraction asserts the pedestrian is on the right side despite the scene description and ground truth indicating a cyclist on the left and the walker on the road, so spatial relations are largely incorrect or missing.
justifications.overall: Significant omissions, incorrect spatial relations, and some attribute errors lead to a low overall quality despite a few correct labels.

justifications.completeness: Captured both pedestrians but missed all annotated vehicles and omitted or mismatched several person attributes from the ground truth.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides many semantic fields aligned with the annotation schema but several key attribute values are incorrect (e.g., ages, atomic actions, communicative/transporting).
justifications.spatial_relations: Only a single spatial relation is given for one pedestrian while the other has none, yielding sparse and potentially spurious spatial information compared to the ground truth.
justifications.overall: Moderate person-level detection but multiple incorrect attribute assignments and the complete omission of all vehicles result in a low overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Extraction misses the single annotated person and most vehicles (only one vehicle reported), so it is largely incomplete.
justifications.semantic_richness: The output gives some attributes for one vehicle and a brief scene description but omits pedestrian attributes and motion details for most vehicles, providing limited semantic information.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes a single spatial relation ('in the distance') that may match the described van but fails to locate other objects, so it is only partially informative.
justifications.overall: The extraction is sparse and misses key entities and motion states, resulting in a low overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Misses the single person (bicyclist) and most vehicles present, detecting only one vehicle.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides some vehicle attributes (trunk/doors, motion) but lacks person attributes, object counts, and details for other vehicles.
justifications.spatial_relations: Reports a single relation ('parked in the distance') that may correspond to one parked car but omits spatial context for other vehicles and the moving vehicle.
justifications.overall: Partial recognition of a parked vehicle with some attributes but major omissions (the bicyclist and multiple vehicles) lead to low overall performance.

justifications.completeness: The extraction captures only 2 pedestrians and no vehicles while the ground truth lists 7 persons and 3 vehicles, so many agents are missing.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction provides multiple semantic attributes (actions, communicative, age, spatial_relations) but many labels are incorrect or incomplete compared with ground truth (e.g., walking vs standing, missing carrying/pushing).
justifications.spatial_relations: Some spatial relations are provided for one pedestrian (on crosswalk, near traffic cone) but others are missing or inconsistent and vehicle relations are omitted.
justifications.overall: The output shows some useful semantic detail but suffers from low coverage and several incorrect or missing attribute assignments, yielding poor overall alignment with ground truth.

justifications.completeness: Detected 1 of 11 relevant agents (8 pedestrians and 3 vehicles), missing all vehicles and most pedestrians.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides a few attribute labels like age and transporting but many fields are generic or incorrect and several communicative and transport details from the ground truth are missing.
justifications.spatial_relations: Only a single spatial relation is reported and it fails to capture the variety of on-road, sidewalk, and jaywalking positions present in the ground truth.
justifications.overall: Sparse and partially incorrect extraction that misses most agents and key attributes, yielding low utility for driving perception.

justifications.completeness: Only one pedestrian is detected and no vehicles are reported despite seven people and one vehicle in the ground truth, so coverage is very poor.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction lists multiple attribute fields but covers only a single subject and contains incorrect action/context labels, giving limited and partly incorrect semantic information.
justifications.spatial_relations: A relation to traffic cones is provided which may be correct, but spatial relations are missing for nearly all pedestrians and the vehicle, reducing usefulness.
justifications.overall: The output includes some scene-level and attribute data but misses most actors and has inaccurate activity/context labels, resulting in low overall quality.

justifications.completeness: The extraction captures a crosswalk sign but misses all annotated pedestrians and vehicles present in the frame.
justifications.semantic_richness: The output contains only a brief sign description and no pedestrian actions, attributes, or vehicle states from the ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: There is no spatial relation information between pedestrians, vehicles, and the crosswalk aside from noting a sign ahead.
justifications.overall: The extraction is largely incomplete and lacks semantic detail and spatial relations, with only a single contextual cue about a crosswalk sign.

justifications.completeness: Detected only one pedestrian and no vehicles while ground truth contains seven people and three vehicles, so the extraction is highly incomplete.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provided basic labels for one pedestrian such as action, context, and age but missed many actors, transport attributes, and some contextual labels and also mischaracterized scene location.
justifications.spatial_relations: Included a single relation near police officer but omitted relations for the majority of persons and no vehicle spatial relations, so coverage is poor.
justifications.overall: Overall the extraction is sparse and incomplete with some correct atomic labels for one person but major omissions of actors, vehicles, and contextual details.

justifications.completeness: Missing most annotated persons and both vehicles, reporting only two pedestrians with no vehicle detections.
justifications.semantic_richness: Many attribute labels are incorrect or inconsistent with ground truth (wrong ages, actions, and transporting), showing low attribute accuracy.
justifications.spatial_relations: Provides only one vague spatial relation and omits positional context for other people and all vehicles, so spatial coverage is poor.
justifications.overall: Overall the extraction is incomplete and contains multiple semantic errors, yielding low reliability for driving perception tasks.

justifications.completeness: Only one pedestrian was detected and no vehicles were reported while the ground truth contains seven persons and four vehicles, so most entities are missing.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction provides several attribute fields but many attributes are incorrect or incomplete relative to ground truth (eg wrong atomic action and missing transporting/communicative details for other people).
justifications.spatial_relations: A single spatial relation was given for the detected person but the extraction misses other spatial relations and relative placements for the majority of pedestrians and all vehicles.
justifications.overall: The output is sparse and partially incorrect, missing most actors and many key attributes, yielding low overall utility for perception evaluation.

justifications.completeness: Detected only one pedestrian out of seven and omitted all vehicles and many person-level attributes, so coverage is very low.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides some attributes (action, context, age) but contains errors (mislabelled crossing, typo) and misses key transport and activity variations present in the ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes an on-the-road relation that matches several ground-truth persons but fails to provide relations for most individuals and asserts an unverified near-traffic-officer relation.
justifications.overall: Overall the extraction is sparse and partially correct in places but has significant omissions and inaccuracies, leading to low reliability for driving-scene understanding.

justifications.completeness: Detected only two pedestrians and no vehicles, missing multiple annotated persons and all vehicles from the ground truth.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides many attribute fields but contains several incorrect or inconsistent labels compared to ground truth (wrong actions, transporting, and ages).
justifications.spatial_relations: Only a single vague spatial relation is provided while most actors lack spatial relations and several detections are missing, making spatial information sparse and unreliable.
justifications.overall: Overall performance is low due to missing actors and multiple incorrect semantic labels, limiting its utility for driving perception tasks.

justifications.completeness: Detected 1 of 5 pedestrians and no vehicles, missing most objects and many annotated attributes.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides basic labels for a single pedestrian and a brief scene description but omits multiple actions, transporting states (e.g., pushing) and all vehicle details, and includes an unsupported claim about a traffic officer directing traffic.
justifications.spatial_relations: Correctly labels one pedestrian as on the sidewalk (matching the ground truth for track 14) but fails to represent road-side walking and other spatial relations for remaining persons and vehicles.
justifications.overall: Overall the extraction is sparse and only partially correct for a single pedestrian while missing most actors, vehicle information, and several key semantic attributes.

justifications.completeness: The extraction reports one pedestrian and no vehicles while the ground truth contains five persons and four vehicles, so most actors are missing.
justifications.semantic_richness: Labels are limited and partially incorrect (eg crossing vs standing/walking/sitting), and the extraction omits transporting and many contextual attributes present in the ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: The single spatial relation 'on the road' matches one ground truth context but the extraction fails to capture other relations such as along the side of the road or biking locations.
justifications.overall: The output is sparse and contains inaccuracies, missing most actors and many important semantic and spatial details from the ground truth.

justifications.completeness: The extraction omits all seven annotated pedestrians and the single parked vehicle, offering only a generic scene label and thus very low coverage.
justifications.semantic_richness: The output provides a brief environmental description but none of the semantic attributes from the ground truth such as actions, transporting, motion status, or ages.
justifications.spatial_relations: Although the description mentions buildings on both sides and trees on the left, it contains no spatial positioning or relations for the pedestrians or the parked vehicle in the ground truth.
justifications.overall: Because the model failed to detect any annotated actors or vehicle and provided minimal scene detail, the overall usefulness for driving perception is very low.

justifications.completeness: Detected one pedestrian and no vehicles whereas ground truth lists six persons and four vehicles, so many objects are missing.
justifications.semantic_richness: Attributes are partially present but several are incorrect or hallucinated (e.g., 'crossing' vs ground truth 'walking', 'carrying with both hands' vs 'none of the above').
justifications.spatial_relations: Provides a single relation ('being directed by traffic officer') that is not supported in the annotations and omits relations among multiple pedestrians and vehicles.
justifications.overall: The extraction captures one plausible actor but misses most agents, mislabels actions or transport states, and includes at least one hallucinated relation, resulting in low overall quality.

justifications.completeness: The extraction captures a single bicycle but misses all seven annotated people and the three annotated vehicles, so it is largely incomplete.
justifications.semantic_richness: The output provides very limited semantics (a brief scene description and one bicycle with motion) and omits most attributes like person actions, counts, ages, and vehicle states.
justifications.spatial_relations: Aside from the vague phrase 'riding ahead', the extraction contains no explicit spatial relationships between actors or relative positions present in the ground truth.
justifications.overall: Given the large number of missed entities and sparse attributes, the extraction provides only minimal and unreliable scene understanding.

justifications.completeness: The extraction completely omits all annotated persons and vehicles, reporting only a few background elements.
justifications.semantic_richness: While the scene description mentions cones, a white line, and building text, it lacks any object-level labels, actions, ages, or vehicle states present in the ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: Some coarse left/right relations for cones and buildings are provided, but there are no spatial relations involving the many pedestrians and vehicles in the scene.
justifications.overall: Given the complete omission of dynamic agents and critical object attributes despite limited background detail, the extraction is largely inadequate for driving perception needs.

justifications.completeness: The VLM detected no pedestrians or vehicles while the ground truth contains five persons and two vehicles, so it failed to report any required objects.
justifications.semantic_richness: Only a generic scene description was provided and no semantic attributes from the ground truth (actions, ages, motion states, door/trunk status) were reported.
justifications.spatial_relations: No spatial relationships or object positioning relative to the road or buildings were reported.
justifications.overall: The extraction provides a vague scene description but omits virtually all annotated entities and their attributes, resulting in an almost entirely unuseful output for perception purposes.

justifications.completeness: Misses all annotated persons and vehicles, only describes a few static scene elements.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides a brief scene description but omits nearly all object attributes, actions, and identities present in the ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: Mentions left/right placement of cones and a white line, but fails to report spatial relations of the many pedestrians and parked vehicles in the frame.
justifications.overall: Extraction captures some static contextual details but largely fails to detect or describe the annotated people and vehicles, resulting in limited usefulness for driving perception.

justifications.completeness: The extraction misses all pedestrians and most vehicles from the ground truth, reporting only a single distant van so coverage is very low.
justifications.semantic_richness: The VLM provides a few attributes for the detected van (motion_status, trunk_open, doors_open, spatial_relations) but omits attributes for the many pedestrians and other vehicles, resulting in limited semantic detail.
justifications.spatial_relations: The model gives a vague relation ('in the distance') for one vehicle but fails to describe relative positions of multiple parked vehicles and seven pedestrians, so spatial relation coverage is poor.
justifications.overall: Overall the extraction is weak due to major misses of pedestrians and most vehicles, with only minimal and imprecise information provided for a single object.

justifications.completeness: The extraction correctly describes the single vehicle and cones but omits all seven annotated pedestrians, so it is largely incomplete.
justifications.semantic_richness: The VLM captures vehicle attributes and a brief scene description but lacks the rich pedestrian semantics (actions, contexts, ages, carrying) present in the ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: It correctly states the truck is on the left side of the road but fails to provide spatial relations for the multiple pedestrians noted in the annotations.
justifications.overall: Despite accurate vehicle details, the complete omission of all pedestrians and their attributes makes the extraction insufficient for driving-scene understanding.

justifications.completeness: The extraction correctly identifies the parked vehicle but completely omits all five pedestrians and their actions, so it is largely incomplete.
justifications.semantic_richness: It provides basic vehicle attributes (type, motion, trunk/doors) but lacks any pedestrian attributes, actions, ages, or carrying information from the ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: The VLM correctly states the truck is on the left side of the road and mentions lane markings, but it fails to localize or relate the multiple pedestrians present.
justifications.overall: Despite correctly reporting a parked truck, missing all pedestrians and most semantic details makes the overall extraction poor.

justifications.completeness: Detected only one of three pedestrians and reported an incorrect vehicle door state, so many entities are missing or wrong.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provided multiple attribute types (action, context, age, vehicle properties) for detected objects but missed the senior person and mis-labeled the vehicle doors.
justifications.spatial_relations: Gave a spatial relation for the lone detected pedestrian that could correspond to one ground-truth person, but omitted relations for the other pedestrians and the vehicle.
justifications.overall: Some attributes are correct, but the extraction is incomplete and contains critical errors (missing pedestrians and wrong vehicle door), resulting in low overall quality.

justifications.completeness: The extraction reports only one pedestrian while the ground truth contains five persons, so it misses the majority of targets.
justifications.semantic_richness: The reported pedestrian has multiple attribute labels but the extraction omits several other individuals and misses a communicative label present in the ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: The extraction correctly identifies proximity to orange traffic cones for the detected person but does not provide spatial relations for the other persons or more detailed relations.
justifications.overall: While attributes for the single detected pedestrian are reasonable, the extraction is largely incomplete and omits important actors and labels from the scene.

justifications.completeness: Detected 1 pedestrian while ground truth contains 6 persons, so the extraction misses most actors and vehicle information.
justifications.semantic_richness: Includes multiple attribute fields for the single detected person but fails to capture the variety of actions and communicative cues present in the ground truth such as talking on the phone and exiting a building.
justifications.spatial_relations: Provided reasonable spatial relations for the detected pedestrian (on the road and near traffic cones) but omitted relations for the other people and finer distinctions like along the side versus on the road.
justifications.overall: The extraction is accurate for one actor but is largely incomplete across the scene and misses multiple actors and important attributes, yielding low overall utility.

justifications.completeness: Only 1 of 5 ground truth persons was detected and no additional actors were found, so coverage is very low.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction records basic attributes (action, context, age) for one pedestrian but misses communicative and transporting cues and ignores most other people.
justifications.spatial_relations: The provided spatial relation 'approaching a store' is not supported by the ground truth and correct relations like entering a building were omitted, indicating inaccurate spatial reasoning.
justifications.overall: The output is sparse and partly correct for a single pedestrian but fails to detect the majority of actors and several key attributes and relations.

justifications.completeness: The extraction detects only one pedestrian out of five annotated and omits several people entirely, so it is largely incomplete.
justifications.semantic_richness: It captures a few attributes (action, simple context, carrying, age) for one subject but misses many per-person attributes, communicative cues, and complex contexts across other pedestrians.
justifications.spatial_relations: A specific relation ('on the left side of the road') is provided for the lone detected pedestrian but spatial relations are absent for the other four people and the claim is not verified by the annotations.
justifications.overall: The extraction provides some correct attributes for a single person but misses most entities and relations, resulting in low overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Most annotated people and vehicles are missing from the extraction, so coverage is very low.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction provides a useful scene-level description (cones, sidewalk, fence, buildings, trees) but omits entity-level attributes, actions, and counts present in the ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: Some coarse spatial relations are described (cones on one side, sidewalk with fence on the other) but relations involving pedestrians and parked vehicles are absent.
justifications.overall: While the scene context is partially captured, the failure to detect any of the pedestrians or vehicles from the ground truth results in a low overall quality.

justifications.completeness: The extraction reports no pedestrians or vehicles despite five persons and seven parked vehicles in the ground truth, so coverage is missing.
justifications.semantic_richness: The scene description mentions cones, a white line, and bicycles but omits most semantic attributes such as pedestrian actions, communicative states, counts, and vehicle motion statuses.
justifications.spatial_relations: The extraction captures basic spatial cues (cones on the left, bicycles on the right, white line center) but fails to relate people and vehicles spatially or provide detailed positional context.
justifications.overall: The output gives limited contextual detail but fails to detect or describe any required pedestrians or vehicles and lacks essential semantic attributes, resulting in poor overall performance.

justifications.completeness: Misses all four annotated pedestrians and their attributes while only describing some static scene elements, so completeness is very low.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides minimal semantic detail (a brief scene description) and omits all person-level semantics such as actions, communicative behavior, and transporting, so richness is near zero.
justifications.spatial_relations: Correctly describes left/right placement of cones and building relative to the road but fails to spatially locate or relate any annotated pedestrians, yielding limited spatial coverage.
justifications.overall: Overall performance is poor because the model omitted all annotated people and their rich attributes despite capturing a few static scene elements.

justifications.completeness: The extraction entirely omits the two annotated persons and therefore misses all person-level annotations.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction provides only a brief scene description and no person attributes, actions, or contextual details that appear in the ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: Although it mentions left/right scene elements, it contains no spatial relations involving the annotated people or their location on the road.
justifications.overall: Overall the extraction fails to detect or describe the annotated persons and lacks the semantic detail needed for useful perception outputs.

justifications.completeness: Misses the single pedestrian and the majority of vehicles, reporting only two objects out of seven and omitting the person entirely.
justifications.semantic_richness: Includes useful attributes like type, motion status, and door/trunk states for reported objects but leaves many fields null and mislabels at least one vehicle motion.
justifications.spatial_relations: Provides specific spatial phrases for two objects but these are sparse, not corroborated by the ground truth, and do not cover most scene objects.
justifications.overall: Sparse and partially incorrect extraction that misses key actors and many vehicles, with some useful attribute reporting but limited reliability for downstream use.

justifications.completeness: Many ground-truth entities are missing (the pedestrian and several vehicles), so the extraction is highly incomplete.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction includes some attributes (trunk/doors, motion) for a subset of objects but omits key semantics for the pedestrian and most vehicles.
justifications.spatial_relations: A few spatial relations are provided but they are sparse and do not cover the majority of vehicles or the pedestrian context.
justifications.overall: Overall the output is only partially informative due to missing entities and at least one incorrect motion label, limiting its usefulness for scene understanding.

justifications.completeness: Extraction omits all annotated entities (two persons and three vehicles) and thus is essentially incomplete.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides a generic scene-level description (buildings, narrow street) but omits all per-object semantics like actions, ages, and vehicle states present in the ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: Mentions buildings on both sides and a street but fails to capture spatial relations involving the people and vehicles from the ground truth (e.g., person walking along the side).
justifications.overall: Overall the extraction captures scene context but misses critical object detections and semantics, resulting in a very low overall score.

justifications.completeness: The extraction missed all annotated people and vehicles, providing no detected pedestrians or vehicles.
justifications.semantic_richness: It contains a basic scene description but lacks detailed semantic attributes like actions, ages, or vehicle states present in the ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: It mentions buildings on both sides and a distant skyscraper but omits spatial relations of people and vehicles relative to the road or each other.
justifications.overall: The output gives a minimal scene-level caption but fails to detect or describe any annotated actors or vehicle states, resulting in low overall utility for perception tasks.

justifications.completeness: Detected only one of two people and no vehicles, missing major entities and contexts.
justifications.semantic_richness: Captures several attributes for one pedestrian but omits the second person and vehicle attributes, limiting overall richness.
justifications.spatial_relations: Provides an unverified spatial relation for the lone detected person and omits spatial info for the missed person and vehicle.
justifications.overall: Partial and somewhat specific extraction but misses key entities and some core attributes, yielding low overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Missed all annotated pedestrians so the extraction fails to report key entities despite correct scene context.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides no attributes or actions for persons and no vehicle details, yielding no semantic richness.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes general scene layout and viewpoint but omits positional relations of the pedestrians relative to the road, so spatial information is limited.
justifications.overall: Overall performance is poor due to missing pedestrians and lack of semantic detail, resulting in a very low score.

justifications.completeness: Misses one of two people and the parked vehicle, and mislabels transporting, so the extraction is largely incomplete.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides many attribute fields (actions, context, transport, age, relations) but several attributes are incorrect or unsupported by the ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes specific relations (near a building, next to a blue cart) which add detail but cannot be fully validated against the ground truth and may be partially incorrect.
justifications.overall: Moderate detail but notable omissions and errors (missing person and vehicle, wrong transport and context) reduce overall quality.

justifications.completeness: The extraction correctly lists the pedestrian and many of their attributes but entirely omits the parked vehicle present in the ground truth, causing a significant omission.
justifications.semantic_richness: The pedestrian attributes are detailed and match the ground truth, but the response lacks vehicle information and object tracking identifiers which reduces overall richness.
justifications.spatial_relations: The provided spatial relation for the pedestrian is plausible and consistent with the scene description, though no spatial relation was given for the omitted vehicle.
justifications.overall: Good coverage and correct pedestrian semantics but omission of the vehicle and missing identifiers lower the overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Captures the annotated person fully but fails to report the parked vehicle present in the ground truth.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides detailed attributes for the pedestrian but lacks vehicle information and additional scene object attributes present in the ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: Gives an accurate spatial relation for the pedestrian relative to the road although no vehicle relation is reported.
justifications.overall: Accurate per-person semantic extraction but omission of the parked vehicle reduces completeness and overall utility.

justifications.completeness: Detected only a single pedestrian and no vehicles, missing the majority of annotated people and both vehicles.
justifications.semantic_richness: Offers multiple semantic fields but several values are incorrect or imprecise (wrong transporting label, typo in context) and it misses many communicative cues.
justifications.spatial_relations: Provides coarse spatial relations that are partially correct (on road, near store) but fails to distinguish side-of-road versus crosswalk positions for most pedestrians.
justifications.overall: Overall the extraction is incomplete and partially inaccurate, giving a single coarse pedestrian description with several wrong attributes and omitting vehicles.

justifications.completeness: The extraction omits all annotated pedestrians and vehicles, capturing only a trivial scene context.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides minimal scene description (crosswalk, tree, bike rack) but lacks any pedestrian or vehicle attributes and actions present in the ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: Mentions left-side tree and bike rack but contains no spatial relations between people, vehicles, or with the crosswalk.
justifications.overall: Due to missing detections of all annotated persons and vehicles, the extraction is largely uninformative despite a brief scene note.

justifications.completeness: Extracted only two pedestrians and no vehicles, missing the large majority of annotated persons and the single vehicle.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides several attribute fields but contains incorrect age/transporting labels and omits many ground-truth context labels like jaywalking and vehicle state.
justifications.spatial_relations: Lists a couple of vague relations but misses many explicit locational contexts (e.g., jaywalking, walking on road) and fails to report the vehicle present.
justifications.overall: Low overall quality due to poor coverage, some incorrect attribute values, and missing vehicle detection despite moderate attribute variety.

justifications.completeness: The extraction reports only two pedestrians and no vehicles while the ground truth lists about 25 persons and 3 vehicles, so coverage is very poor.
justifications.semantic_richness: Some attributes (age, atomic_action) are present but many labels are incorrect or missing (most pedestrians are walking, transporting states are often wrong) and many entities are omitted.
justifications.spatial_relations: Only a single vague spatial relation is provided and the extraction misses critical relations like jaywalking versus on-road positions for many pedestrians.
justifications.overall: Due to very low entity recall and multiple attribute inaccuracies, the overall extraction quality is poor despite limited useful semantic tags.

justifications.completeness: Detected only a single pedestrian and no vehicles while ground truth lists many pedestrians and multiple vehicles, so most entities are missing.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides some descriptive details (age, facing a store, sidewalk) but misses most actors, many actions, transport and communicative states and mislabels walking as standing.
justifications.spatial_relations: Gives two relations ('facing a store', 'near a tree') that are plausible for the described subject but do not cover other pedestrians and may be partially incorrect.
justifications.overall: Overall low quality due to missing most actors and several incorrect or unverified labels, despite a somewhat informative scene description.

justifications.completeness: The extraction lists only one pedestrian and two vehicles while the ground truth contains many more persons and four vehicles, so numerous objects are missing.
justifications.semantic_richness: Some useful attributes are provided such as action, context, age, and vehicle states, but many person-specific labels are incorrect or omitted and several objects lack attributes.
justifications.spatial_relations: A single spatial relation is given and spatial placement for most pedestrians and vehicles is not captured or misaligned with ground truth contexts like crossing at pedestrian crossings.
justifications.overall: The extraction captures a few correct attributes but misses the majority of objects and many scene relations, resulting in low overall quality.

justifications.completeness: The extraction detected only one pedestrian out of many annotated and omitted at least one vehicle, so it is largely incomplete.
justifications.semantic_richness: The output includes several attribute fields (motion, trunk/doors, transporting) but misses or misassigns many ground-truth semantic labels and lacks coverage across instances.
justifications.spatial_relations: A few spatial relations are provided but they are often vague or incorrect and fail to capture important contexts like crossing or jaywalking.
justifications.overall: Overall the extraction is low quality due to missing most pedestrians, incorrect or inconsistent attributes, and inaccurate spatial relations.

justifications.completeness: The extraction captures only one pedestrian and two vehicles versus 14 persons and 4 vehicles in the ground truth, missing most objects.
justifications.semantic_richness: It reports some attributes (action, age, transporting) but misses many context labels, contains an incorrect transporting label, and omits communicative and complex contexts present in the ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: Provides a few coarse relations (near, on the road, next to) but fails to identify specific contexts like pedestrian crossings and jaywalking for multiple pedestrians.
justifications.overall: Overall the extraction is sparse and partially incorrect, recovering few objects and limited semantics relative to the ground truth.

justifications.completeness: Missed one of the two persons and misclassified age and simple context despite correctly detecting the train, so many ground-truth entities/attributes are absent or wrong.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides multiple semantic attributes (action, age, spatial relation, vehicle motion) but includes incorrect age and unsupported trunk/door states.
justifications.spatial_relations: Identifies proximity to the train track correctly but omits relations implied by the bicyclist and mischaracterizes the person's context as roadside sitting.
justifications.overall: Moderate overall: detects key elements (moving train, person near tracks) but misses a second person and contains notable attribute errors that reduce reliability.

justifications.completeness: The VLM found one pedestrian and the train but missed a second person and failed to match key labeled attributes such as the pedestrians' ages and the second person's context.
justifications.semantic_richness: The extraction supplies several semantic attributes and spatial cues but includes incorrect details (age labeled as adult, explicit trunk/door states) and omits or mislabels other ground truth properties.
justifications.spatial_relations: Provided spatial relations like near fence and by train are plausible given the scene description, but they are not corroborated by ground truth annotations and the second pedestrian lacks any spatial relation.
justifications.overall: The output captures some relevant entities and plausible relations but misses an entire person and contains notable attribute errors, yielding limited overall reliability.

justifications.completeness: Extraction misses all annotated persons and the vehicle, providing no corresponding detections.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides a simple scene description but contains none of the detailed attributes such as person ages, actions, or vehicle motion present in the ground truth.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes wall placement relative to the road but contains no spatial relations involving the annotated persons or vehicle.
justifications.overall: Overall the extraction fails to detect any ground truth actors and omits critical semantic attributes, rendering it unsuitable for the annotated driving scene.

justifications.completeness: Captured the bicycle and its motion but failed to include the actual person object and detailed person attributes from the ground truth.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides a useful scene-level description including bridge and graffiti but lacks structured person attributes like age and atomic action, so semantic detail is limited.
justifications.spatial_relations: Includes road and under-bridge relation in the scene description, but misses explicit spatial linking of the person to the bicycle as a tracked pedestrian object.
justifications.overall: Correctly identifies the moving bicycle and some scene context but omits the required person annotation and detailed semantic attributes, yielding a partial but insufficient extraction.

justifications.completeness: The extraction correctly identifies the person and atomic action and labels communicative and transporting attributes, but it mislabels age and simple context compared to the ground truth.
justifications.semantic_richness: The output provides rich descriptive details including scene description and spatial relations beyond the ground truth, but contains a critical error in age classification and a simple-context mismatch.
justifications.spatial_relations: The reported spatial relations (next to a white wall, under a bridge-like structure) align well with the provided scene description and appear accurate.
justifications.overall: The extraction is informative and mostly accurate in action and context but is weakened by an incorrect age label and a simple-context disagreement with the ground truth.

justifications.completeness: The extraction correctly captures the person and atomic action and matches communicative and transporting labels but mislabels age and provides an incorrect simple context compared to ground truth, so it is incomplete.
justifications.semantic_richness: The VLM adds descriptive details (clothing color, wall, tunnel-like area) beyond the ground truth, increasing richness despite the age and context errors.
justifications.spatial_relations: The extraction provides a specific spatial relation not present in the ground truth which may be useful but cannot be validated against the annotations, so it is partially reliable.
justifications.overall: The extraction is partially accurate and informative but has key mismatches (age and simple context) that reduce its overall correctness.

justifications.completeness: The extractor correctly detected the person and atomic action and matched communicative and transporting labels, but it misclassified age and added an incorrect simple_context value.
justifications.semantic_richness: The output contains additional scene context and spatial relations that enrich the description compared to the ground truth, though it includes an incorrect age label.
justifications.spatial_relations: Provided spatial relations (against a white wall, on the ground) are specific and plausible for the frame even though the ground truth does not annotate them for verification.
justifications.overall: Accurate on the core action and several attributes but undermined by the wrong age classification and an erroneous simple_context, yielding moderate overall quality.

justifications.completeness: Captured the person and correct atomic action but missed the ground-truth age and mislabeled the simple context, so key attributes are incomplete.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides richer descriptive context (scene description and spatial relations) than the ground truth but includes an incorrect age and an inaccurate simple context label.
justifications.spatial_relations: Adds plausible spatial relations (next to curb, in tunnel-like area) that align with the scene description, though the ground truth does not annotate spatial relations for direct verification.
justifications.overall: Overall useful and informative extraction with correct action and helpful spatial cues, but reduced reliability due to the incorrect age classification and simple context mismatch.

justifications.completeness: Captured the atomic action and non-communicative/transport labels but missed the annotated age, omitted a track id and mismatched the ground-truth simple context.
justifications.semantic_richness: Provides additional scene description (smoking, tunnel/underpass) and contextual detail beyond the ground truth but contains an incorrect age label and an arguably extraneous simple_context.
justifications.spatial_relations: Accurately reports proximate relations (sitting on the curb, next to a white wall) which align with the scene description and are informative for spatial understanding.
justifications.overall: A moderately strong extraction that correctly identifies the main action and spatial layout and adds useful scene detail, but is weakened by an incorrect age classification and some context mismatches.

justifications.completeness: The extraction captures the road environment but completely omits the single annotated person (senior sitting), so it is largely incomplete.
justifications.semantic_richness: While the scene description is detailed about the environment, it contains no information about the annotated person or their attributes (age, action), so semantic richness is low.
justifications.spatial_relations: Environmental spatial relations (under a bridge, leading to daylight) are described, but the extraction provides no spatial relation involving the annotated person, resulting in partial coverage.
justifications.overall: Given the accurate environment description but the critical omission of the annotated person and their attributes, the overall extraction is poor.
